purpose: Classify antimatter storage as custody risk, not just container design.

failure_modes.description: Stored antimatter contacts ordinary matter boundary and annihilates.
Magnetic/electric confinement state fails or degrades below containment requirement.
Vacuum boundary degrades and increases annihilation probability.
Thermal environment invalidates assumed trap stability.
No continuous object trail proves storage state across transfer or time interval.
A lab or theoretical storage condition is presented as mission or industrial storage.
